title: potentilla alpestris var. friesiana
intro: Potentilla alpestris var. friesiana, also known as Fries's Alpine Cinquefoil, is a plant species in the Rosaceae family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Potentilla alpestris var. friesiana include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to create teas, salves, and other topical treatments.
